explanations.3: This special comment tells Docker to use a newer syntax version (1.4) which enables advanced features like cache mounting.
explanations.26: The --mount flag caches the apt package lists between builds so Docker doesn't re-download everything; the id and sharing options control how that cache is managed.
explanations.27: The 'set -eux' command makes the shell script strict: exit on errors, show each command being run, and fail if any piped command fails.
explanations.42: This creates a package.json file inline using echo rather than copying it, which is a lightweight way to define Node dependencies without a separate file.
explanations.48: Similar to line 26, this mounts the Composer cache directory so dependency downloads are reused between builds.
explanations.54: This starts a new stage called 'php' from a fresh FPM image; previous 'builder' stage files won't be in the final image unless explicitly copied.
explanations.78: The --gid flag creates a group with a specific numeric ID; this ensures the group has the same ID inside and outside the container for file permissions.
explanations.79: The --password '' sets an empty password, --gid links the user to the group created above, and -l prevents the user from being added to the login database.
explanations.84: The --from=builder syntax copies files from the earlier builder stage, and --chown ensures the copied files are owned by our new user.
explanations.89: This adds the node_modules bin directory to PATH so npm packages' executable commands can be run directly without typing the full path.
